<content>1: import type { DiffStats, LLMSummary, ConfidenceLevel, SummaryAnnotation } from "../types";
2: import type { RepoTaskRunner } from "./runner";
3: import { buildSummaryPrompt, validateSummaryOutput } from "./tasks/summary";
4: 
5: /**
6:  * Summary generator interface — allows swapping in a real LLM backend later.
7:  */
8: export interface SummaryGenerator {
9:   generate(params: SummaryInput, onLog?: (line: string) => void): Promise<LLMSummary>;
10: }
11: 
12: export interface SummaryInput {
13:   repo: string;
14:   branch: string;
15:   baseRef: string;
16:   headRef: string;
17:   diff: string;
18:   diffStats: DiffStats;
19:   confidence: ConfidenceLevel;
20:   commitMessages: string[];
21: }
22: 
23: /**
24:  * V1 stub: generates structured summaries from diff stats and commit messages.
25:  * No LLM call — deterministic output from heuristics.
26:  * Swap this for a real LLM-backed implementation in Phase 3.
27:  */
28: export class StubSummaryGenerator implements SummaryGenerator {
29:   async generate(params: SummaryInput): Promise<LLMSummary> {
30:     const { diffStats, confidence, commitMessages } = params;
31: 
32:     const totalLines = diffStats.additions + diffStats.deletions;
33:     const fileList = diffStats.files.map((f) => f.filename);
34:     const modules = extractModules(fileList);
35: 
36:     const what = commitMessages.length > 0
37:       ? commitMessages.join("; ")
38:       : `${diffStats.files_changed} files changed (+${diffStats.additions}/-${diffStats.deletions})`;
39: 
40:     const risk = buildRiskAssessment(diffStats, confidence);
41:     const action = mapConfidenceToAction(confidence);
42:     const annotations = buildStubAnnotations(diffStats);
43: 
44:     return {
45:       title: `${params.branch}: ${diffStats.files_changed} files changed`,
46:       what_changed: what,
47:       risk_assessment: risk,
48:       affected_modules: modules,
49:       recommended_action: action,
50:       annotations,
51:     };
52:   }
53: }
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Runs Codex as an agent inside a Docker container with the full codebase.
57:  * Delegates Docker lifecycle to RepoTaskRunner; handles prompt building and output validation.
58:  */
59: export class CodexSummaryGenerator implements SummaryGenerator {
60:   constructor(private runner: RepoTaskRunner) {}
61: 
62:   async generate(params: SummaryInput, onLog?: (line: string) => void): Promise<LLMSummary> {
63:     const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(params);
64: 
65:     const result = await this.runner.run({
66:       repo: params.repo,
67:       baseRef: params.baseRef,
68:       headRef: params.headRef,
69:       prompt,
70:       onLog,
71:     });
72: 
73:     if (!result.ok) {
74:       throw new Error(`Codex runner failed (${result.durationMs}ms): ${result.logs.slice(0, 500)}`);
75:     }
76: 
77:     return validateSummaryOutput(result.output);
78:   }
79: }
80: 
81: /**
82:  * Build annotations from diff stats by grouping files by status.
83:  */
84: function buildStubAnnotations(diffStats: DiffStats): SummaryAnnotation[] {
85:   const groups: Record<string, { type: SummaryAnnotation["type"]; files: string[] }> = {};
86: 
87:   for (const f of diffStats.files) {
88:     let type: SummaryAnnotation["type"];
89:     let label: string;
90:     switch (f.status) {
91:       case "added":
92:         type = "new_module";
93:         label = "added";
94:         break;
95:       case "deleted":
96:         type = "refactor";
97:         label = "deleted";
98:         break;
99:       default:
100:         type = "change";
101:         label = "modified";
102:         break;
103:     }
104:     if (!groups[label]) groups[label] = { type, files: [] };
105:     groups[label].files.push(f.filename);
106:   }
107: 
108:   const annotations: SummaryAnnotation[] = [];
109:   for (const [label, { type, files }] of Object.entries(groups)) {
110:     annotations.push({
111:       text: `${files.length} file(s) ${label}`,
112:       files,
113:       type,
114:     });
115:   }
116:   return annotations;
117: }
118: 
119: /**
120:  * Extract top-level directory modules from file paths.
121:  * e.g. ["src/api/webhooks.ts", "src/db/schema.ts"] → ["src/api", "src/db"]
122:  */
123: function extractModules(files: string[]): string[] {
124:   const modules = new Set<string>();
125:   for (const f of files) {
126:     const parts = f.split("/");
127:     if (parts.length >= 2) {
128:       modules.add(parts.slice(0, 2).join("/"));
129:     } else {
130:       modules.add(parts[0]);
131:     }
132:   }
133:   return [...modules].sort();
134: }
135: 
136: function buildRiskAssessment(diff: DiffStats, confidence: ConfidenceLevel): string {
137:   const totalLines = diff.additions + diff.deletions;
138:   const parts: string[] = [];
139: 
140:   if (confidence === "critical") {
141:     parts.push("High-risk change.");
142:   } else if (confidence === "needs_review") {
143:     parts.push("Moderate-risk change.");
144:   } else {
145:     parts.push("Low-risk change.");
146:   }
147: 
148:   parts.push(`${totalLines} lines across ${diff.files_changed} files.`);
149: 
150:   const deletionRatio = diff.deletions / (totalLines || 1);
151:   if (deletionRatio > 0.7) {
152:     parts.push("Primarily deletions — verify nothing critical was removed.");
153:   }
154: 
155:   const addedFiles = diff.files.filter((f) => f.status === "added");
156:   if (addedFiles.length > 0) {
157:     parts.push(`${addedFiles.length} new file(s) added.`);
158:   }
159: 
160:   return parts.join(" ");
161: }
162: 
163: function mapConfidenceToAction(confidence: ConfidenceLevel): LLMSummary["recommended_action"] {
164:   switch (confidence) {
165:     case "safe": return "approve";
166:     case "needs_review": return "review";
167:     case "critical": return "block";
168:   }
169: }

<content>1: import type { ChangeQueries, EventQueries, JobQueries } from "../db/queries";
2: import type { ForgejoClient } from "../forgejo/client";
3: import type { SummaryGenerator, SummaryInput } from "../engine/summary";
4: import type { Change, DiffStats, Job, NotificationConfig } from "../types";
5: import type { LogBus } from "../engine/log-bus";
6: import { ScoringEngine } from "../engine/review";
7: import { PolicyEngine } from "../engine/policy";
8: import { ChangeStateMachine } from "../engine/state-machine";
9: import { NotificationSender } from "./notify";
10: 
11: export interface WorkerDeps {
12:   changes: ChangeQueries;
13:   events: EventQueries;
14:   jobs: JobQueries;
15:   forgejo: ForgejoClient;
16:   scorer: ScoringEngine;
17:   policy: PolicyEngine;
18:   summary: SummaryGenerator;
19:   stateMachine: ChangeStateMachine;
20:   notifier: NotificationSender;
21:   notificationConfigs: NotificationConfig[];
22:   logBus?: LogBus;
23: }
24: 
25: export interface WorkerConfig {
26:   /** Poll interval in ms. Default: 1000 */
27:   pollInterval: number;
28:   /** Commit status context name. Default: "redc" */
29:   statusContext: string;
30:   /** Git remote name to fetch after merging. Null to skip. */
31:   fetchRemoteAfterMerge: string | null;
32: }
33: 
34: const DEFAULT_CONFIG: WorkerConfig = {
35:   pollInterval: 1000,
36:   statusContext: "redc",
37:   fetchRemoteAfterMerge: null,
38: };
39: 
40: /**
41:  * Job worker — polls the SQLite job queue and processes scoring/summary jobs.
42:  *
43:  * Job types:
44:  *   - score_change: fetch diff → score → set commit status → enqueue summary
45:  *   - generate_summary: generate LLM summary → transition to ready_for_review
46:  *   - send_notification: deliver webhook/slack notifications
47:  *   - approve_change: auto-approve and enqueue merge
48:  *   - merge_change: create PR if needed, merge, transition to merged
49:  */
50: export class JobWorker {
51:   private config: WorkerConfig;
52:   private running = false;
53:   private timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
54: 
55:   constructor(
56:     private deps: WorkerDeps,
57:     config: Partial<WorkerConfig> = {}
58:   ) {
59:     this.config = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };
60:   }
61: 
62:   start(): void {
63:     if (this.running) return;
64:     this.running = true;
65:     this.poll();
66:   }
67: 
68:   stop(): void {
69:     this.running = false;
70:     if (this.timer) {
71:       clearTimeout(this.timer);
72:       this.timer = null;
73:     }
74:   }
75: 
76:   /** Process a single job cycle. Exposed for testing. */
77:   async tick(): Promise<Job | null> {
78:     const job = this.deps.jobs.claimNext();
79:     if (!job) return null;
80: 
81:     try {
82:       switch (job.type) {
83:         case "score_change":
84:           await this.handleScoreChange(job);
85:           break;
86:         case "generate_summary":
87:           await this.handleGenerateSummary(job);
88:           break;
89:         case "send_notification":
90:           await this.handleSendNotification(job);
91:           break;
92:         case "approve_change":
93:           await this.handleApproveChange(job);
94:           break;
95:         case "merge_change":
96:           await this.handleMergeChange(job);
97:           break;
98:         default:
99:           throw new Error(`Unknown job type: ${job.type}`);
100:       }
101:       this.deps.jobs.complete(job.id);
102:     } catch (err) {
103:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
104:       this.deps.jobs.fail(job.id, message);
105:     }
106: 
107:     return job;
108:   }
109: 
110:   private poll(): void {
111:     if (!this.running) return;
112:     this.tick().finally(() => {
113:       if (this.running) {
114:         this.timer = setTimeout(() => this.poll(), this.config.pollInterval);
115:       }
116:     });
117:   }
118: 
119:   private async handleScoreChange(job: Job): Promise<void> {
120:     const { change_id } = JSON.parse(job.payload) as { change_id: number };
121:     const change = this.deps.changes.getById(change_id);
122:     if (!change) throw new Error(`Change ${change_id} not found`);
123:     if (change.status === "superseded") return; // skip superseded
124: 
125:     // Parse repo owner/name
126:     const [owner, repo] = parseRepoFullName(change.repo);
127: 
128:     // Transition to scoring
129:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "scoring");
130: 
131:     // Set commit status to pending
132:     await this.deps.forgejo.setCommitStatus(owner, repo, change.head_sha, {
133:       state: "pending",
134:       description: "Scoring change...",
135:       context: this.config.statusContext,
136:     });
137: 
138:     // Fetch diff stats
139:     const diffStats = await this.deps.forgejo.compareDiff(
140:       owner, repo, change.base_branch, change.head_sha
141:     );
142: 
143:     // Score
144:     const result = this.deps.scorer.score(diffStats);
145:     this.deps.changes.updateConfidence(change_id, result.confidence);
146:     this.deps.changes.updateDiffStats(change_id, JSON.stringify(diffStats));
147: 
148:     // Transition to scored via state machine
149:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "scored", {
150:       confidence: result.confidence,
151:       reasons: result.reasons,
152:     });
153: 
154:     // Evaluate policy
155:     const policy = await this.deps.policy.loadPolicy(owner, repo, change.base_branch);
156:     const decision = this.deps.policy.evaluate(policy, diffStats, result.confidence);
157: 
158:     // Set commit status based on scoring
159:     const statusState = result.confidence === "safe" ? "success" as const
160:       : result.confidence === "critical" ? "failure" as const
161:       : "pending" as const;
162: 
163:     await this.deps.forgejo.setCommitStatus(owner, repo, change.head_sha, {
164:       state: statusState,
165:       description: `${result.confidence}: ${result.reasons[0]}`,
166:       context: this.config.statusContext,
167:     });
168: 
169:     // Transition to summarizing and enqueue summary job
170:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "summarizing");
171:     this.deps.jobs.enqueue({
172:       org_id: change.org_id,
173:       type: "generate_summary",
174:       payload: JSON.stringify({
175:         change_id,
176:         diff_stats: diffStats,
177:         policy_decision: decision,
178:       }),
179:     });
180:   }
181: 
182:   private async handleGenerateSummary(job: Job): Promise<void> {
183:     const payload = JSON.parse(job.payload) as {
184:       change_id: number;
185:       diff_stats: DiffStats;
186:       policy_decision: { action: string };
187:     };
188: 
189:     const change = this.deps.changes.getById(payload.change_id);
190:     if (!change) throw new Error(`Change ${payload.change_id} not found`);
191:     if (change.status === "superseded") return;
192: 
193:     const [owner, repo] = parseRepoFullName(change.repo);
194: 
195:     // Fetch the actual diff text for the summary generator
196:     const diff = await this.deps.forgejo.getDiff(
197:       owner, repo, change.base_branch, change.head_sha
198:     );
199: 
200:     // Get commit messages from change events or use a placeholder
201:     const events = this.deps.events.listByChangeId(change.id);
202:     const pushEvent = events.find((e) => e.event_type === "push_received");
203:     const commitMessages = pushEvent?.metadata
204:       ? [JSON.parse(pushEvent.metadata).commits + " commit(s)"]
205:       : [];
206: 
207:     const input: SummaryInput = {
208:       repo: change.repo,
209:       branch: change.branch,
210:       baseRef: change.base_branch,
211:       headRef: change.head_sha,
212:       diff,
213:       diffStats: payload.diff_stats,
214:       confidence: change.confidence!,
215:       commitMessages,
216:     };
217: 
218:     const onLog = this.deps.logBus
219:       ? (line: string) => this.deps.logBus!.emit(change.id, line)
220:       : undefined;
221: 
222:     let summary;
223:     try {
224:       summary = await this.deps.summary.generate(input, onLog);
225:     } finally {
226:       this.deps.logBus?.complete(change.id);
227:     }
228: 
229:     // Store summary
230:     this.deps.changes.updateSummary(change.id, JSON.stringify(summary));
231: 
232:     // Transition to ready_for_review
233:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change.id, "ready_for_review");
234: 
235:     // Log summary event
236:     this.deps.events.append({
237:       change_id: change.id,
238:       event_type: "summary_generated",
239:       from_status: "summarizing",
240:       to_status: "ready_for_review",
241:       metadata: JSON.stringify({ recommended_action: summary.recommended_action }),
242:     });
243: 
244:     // Enqueue notification if configs exist
245:     if (this.deps.notificationConfigs.length > 0) {
246:       const event = change.confidence === "critical" ? "change_critical" : "change_ready";
247:       this.deps.jobs.enqueue({
248:         org_id: change.org_id,
249:         type: "send_notification",
250:         payload: JSON.stringify({ change_id: change.id, event }),
251:       });
252:     }
253: 
254:     // Auto-approve if policy says so
255:     if (payload.policy_decision?.action === "auto-approve") {
256:       this.deps.jobs.enqueue({
257:         org_id: change.org_id,
258:         type: "approve_change",
259:         payload: JSON.stringify({
260:           change_id: change.id,
261:           policy_decision: payload.policy_decision,
262:         }),
263:       });
264:     }
265:   }
266: 
267:   private async handleApproveChange(job: Job): Promise<void> {
268:     const { change_id, policy_decision } = JSON.parse(job.payload) as {
269:       change_id: number;
270:       policy_decision: { action: string };
271:     };
272: 
273:     const change = this.deps.changes.getById(change_id);
274:     if (!change) throw new Error(`Change ${change_id} not found`);
275:     if (change.status === "superseded") return;
276: 
277:     // Only auto-approve; anything else stays at ready_for_review for manual review
278:     if (policy_decision.action !== "auto-approve") {
279:       this.deps.events.append({
280:         change_id,
281:         event_type: "approval_skipped",
282:         metadata: JSON.stringify({ reason: "not auto-approve", action: policy_decision.action }),
283:       });
284:       return;
285:     }
286: 
287:     const [owner, repo] = parseRepoFullName(change.repo);
288: 
289:     // Transition → approved
290:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "approved");
291: 
292:     // Set commit status to success
293:     await this.deps.forgejo.setCommitStatus(owner, repo, change.head_sha, {
294:       state: "success",
295:       description: "Approved by redc",
296:       context: this.config.statusContext,
297:     });
298: 
299:     // Log approval event
300:     this.deps.events.append({
301:       change_id,
302:       event_type: "change_approved",
303:       from_status: "ready_for_review",
304:       to_status: "approved",
305:       metadata: JSON.stringify({ policy_decision }),
306:     });
307: 
308:     // Enqueue merge
309:     this.deps.jobs.enqueue({
310:       org_id: change.org_id,
311:       type: "merge_change",
312:       payload: JSON.stringify({ change_id }),
313:     });
314:   }
315: 
316:   private async handleMergeChange(job: Job): Promise<void> {
317:     const { change_id } = JSON.parse(job.payload) as { change_id: number };
318: 
319:     const change = this.deps.changes.getById(change_id);
320:     if (!change) throw new Error(`Change ${change_id} not found`);
321:     if (change.status === "superseded") return;
322: 
323:     const [owner, repo] = parseRepoFullName(change.repo);
324: 
325:     // Find or create PR
326:     let prNumber = change.pr_number;
327: 
328:     if (!prNumber) {
329:       // Try to find an existing open PR for this branch
330:       const prs = await this.deps.forgejo.listPRsForBranch(owner, repo, change.branch);
331:       if (prs.length > 0) {
332:         prNumber = prs[0].number;
333:         this.deps.changes.updatePrNumber(change_id, prNumber);
334:       }
335:     }
336: 
337:     if (!prNumber) {
338:       // Create a new PR
339:       const summary = change.summary ? JSON.parse(change.summary) : null;
340:       const title = summary?.title || `Merge ${change.branch}`;
341:       const pr = await this.deps.forgejo.createPR(owner, repo, {
342:         title,
343:         head: change.branch,
344:         base: change.base_branch,
345:         body: summary?.description || undefined,
346:       });
347:       prNumber = pr.number;
348:       this.deps.changes.updatePrNumber(change_id, prNumber);
349:     }
350: 
351:     // Transition → merging
352:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "merging");
353: 
354:     // Merge the PR
355:     try {
356:       await this.deps.forgejo.mergePR(owner, repo, prNumber, "merge");
357:     } catch (err) {
358:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
359:       this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "merge_failed");
360:       this.deps.events.append({
361:         change_id,
362:         event_type: "merge_failed",
363:         from_status: "merging",
364:         to_status: "merge_failed",
365:         metadata: JSON.stringify({ error: message, pr_number: prNumber }),
366:       });
367:       return; // Don't rethrow — the state machine captures the failure
368:     }
369: 
370:     // Transition → merged
371:     this.deps.stateMachine.transition(change_id, "merged");
372: 
373:     // Fetch the updated remote so local refs stay in sync
374:     if (this.config.fetchRemoteAfterMerge) {
375:       try {
376:         const proc = Bun.spawn(["git", "fetch", this.config.fetchRemoteAfterMerge]);
377:         await proc.exited;
378:       } catch {
379:         // Non-fatal — local ref sync is best-effort
380:       }
381:     }
382: 
383:     // Log merge event
384:     this.deps.events.append({
385:       change_id,
386:       event_type: "change_merged",
387:       from_status: "merging",
388:       to_status: "merged",
389:       metadata: JSON.stringify({ pr_number: prNumber }),
390:     });
391: 
392:     // Enqueue notification if configs exist
393:     if (this.deps.notificationConfigs.length > 0) {
394:       this.deps.jobs.enqueue({
395:         org_id: change.org_id,
396:         type: "send_notification",
397:         payload: JSON.stringify({ change_id, event: "change_merged" }),
398:       });
399:     }
400:   }
401: 
402:   private async handleSendNotification(job: Job): Promise<void> {
403:     const { change_id, event } = JSON.parse(job.payload) as {
404:       change_id: number;
405:       event: "change_ready" | "change_critical" | "change_merged";
406:     };
407: 
408:     const change = this.deps.changes.getById(change_id);
409:     if (!change) throw new Error(`Change ${change_id} not found`);
410: 
411:     const results = await this.deps.notifier.send(
412:       this.deps.notificationConfigs,
413:       change,
414:       event
415:     );
416: 
417:     // Log notification results
418:     this.deps.events.append({
419:       change_id,
420:       event_type: "notification_sent",
421:       metadata: JSON.stringify({
422:         event,
423:         results: results.map((r) => ({
424:           url: r.url,
425:           success: r.success,
426:           error: r.error,
427:         })),
428:       }),
429:     });
430:   }
431: }
432: 
433: function parseRepoFullName(fullName: string): [string, string] {
434:   const [owner, ...rest] = fullName.split("/");
435:   return [owner, rest.join("/")];
436: }

2:  * Core domain types for redc.
3:  *
4:  * Change lifecycle state machine:
5:  *
6:  *   pushed → scoring → scored → summarizing → ready_for_review
7:  *                                                 ↓           ↓
8:  *                                             approved    rejected
9:  *                                                 ↓
10:  *                                             merging → merged
11:  *                                                 ↓
12:  *                                              closed
13:  *
14:  *   Any state → superseded (new push to same branch)
15:  */
16: 
17: export type ChangeStatus =
18:   | "pushed"
19:   | "scoring"
20:   | "scored"
21:   | "summarizing"
22:   | "ready_for_review"
23:   | "approved"
24:   | "rejected"
25:   | "merging"
26:   | "merge_failed"
27:   | "merged"
28:   | "closed"
29:   | "superseded";
30: 
31: export type ConfidenceLevel = "safe" | "needs_review" | "critical";
32: 
33: export type CreatedBy = "human" | "agent";
34: 
35: export interface Change {
36:   id: number;
37:   org_id: string;
38:   repo: string;
39:   branch: string;
40:   base_branch: string;
41:   head_sha: string;
42:   pr_number: number | null;
43:   status: ChangeStatus;
44:   confidence: ConfidenceLevel | null;
45:   created_by: CreatedBy;
46:   summary: string | null;
47:   diff_stats: DiffStats | null;
48:   delivery_id: string;
49:   created_at: string;
50:   updated_at: string;
51: }
52: 
53: export interface DiffStats {
54:   files_changed: number;
55:   additions: number;
56:   deletions: number;
57:   files: FileStats[];
58: }
59: 
60: export interface FileStats {
61:   filename: string;
62:   additions: number;
63:   deletions: number;
64:   status: "added" | "modified" | "deleted" | "renamed";
65: }
66: 
67: export interface ChangeEvent {
68:   id: number;
69:   change_id: number;
70:   event_type: string;
71:   from_status: ChangeStatus | null;
72:   to_status: ChangeStatus | null;
73:   metadata: string | null;
74:   created_at: string;
75: }
76: 
77: export interface Job {
78:   id: number;
79:   org_id: string;
80:   type: string;
81:   payload: string;
82:   status: "pending" | "processing" | "completed" | "failed" | "dead";
83:   attempts: number;
84:   max_attempts: number;
85:   last_error: string | null;
86:   run_at: string;
87:   created_at: string;
88:   updated_at: string;
89: }
90: 
91: export interface PolicyRule {
92:   name: string;
93:   match: {
94:     files?: string[];
95:     confidence?: ConfidenceLevel;
96:   };
97:   action: "auto-approve" | "require-review" | "block";
98:   reviewers?: string[];
99: }
100:

102:   rules: PolicyRule[];
103: }
104: 
105: export interface SummaryAnnotation {
106:   text: string;
107:   files: string[];
108:   type: "new_module" | "refactor" | "bugfix" | "config" | "change";
109: }
110: 
111: export interface LLMSummary {
112:   title: string;

28: }

30: export class RepoTaskRunner {
31:   constructor(private config: RepoTaskRunnerConfig) {}
32: 
33:   async run(request: RepoTaskRequest): Promise<RepoTaskResult> {
34:     const timeout = request.timeoutMs ?? this.config.defaultTimeoutMs ?? 120_000;
35:     const repoUrl = `${this.config.forgejoBaseUrl.replace(/\/+$/, "")}/${request.repo}.git`;
36:     const tmpDir = await mkdtemp(join(tmpdir(), "redc-runner-"));
37:     const start = Date.now();
38: 
39:     try {
40:       const args = [
41:         "docker", "run", "--rm",
42:         "-v", `${tmpDir}:/output`,
43:         "-e", `REPO_URL=${repoUrl}`,
44:         "-e", `BASE_REF=${request.baseRef}`,
45:         "-e", `HEAD_REF=${request.headRef}`,
46:         "-e", `TASK_PROMPT=${request.prompt}`,
47:       ];
48: 
49:       if (this.config.openaiApiKey) {
50:         args.push("-e", `OPENAI_API_KEY=${this.config.openaiApiKey}`);
51:       } else {
52:         // Mount host codex auth so the container uses ChatGPT OAuth
53:         const codexDir = join(homedir(), ".codex");
54:         args.push("-v", `${codexDir}:/root/.codex:ro`);
55:       }
56: 
57:       args.push(this.config.image);
58: 
59:       const proc = Bun.spawn(args, {
60:         stdout: "pipe",
61:         stderr: "pipe",
62:       });
63: 
64:       const timer = setTimeout(() => proc.kill(), timeout);
65: 
66:       // Stream stderr line-by-line, collecting full output and calling onLog
67:       const stderrLines: string[] = [];
68:       const stderrDone = (async () => {
69:         const reader = proc.stderr.getReader();
70:         const decoder = new TextDecoder();
71:         let partial = "";
72: 
73:         while (true) {
74:           const { done, value } = await reader.read();
75:           if (done) break;
76:           partial += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
77:           const lines = partial.split("\n");
78:           partial = lines.pop()!; // keep incomplete last line
79:           for (const line of lines) {
80:             stderrLines.push(line);
81:             request.onLog?.(line);
82:           }
83:         }
84:         // Flush remaining partial line
85:         if (partial) {
86:           stderrLines.push(partial);
87:           request.onLog?.(partial);
88:         }
89:       })();
90: 
91:       const [, exitCode] = await Promise.all([stderrDone, proc.exited]);
92:       clearTimeout(timer);
93: 
94:       const stderr = stderrLines.join("\n");
95:       const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
96: 
97:       if (exitCode !== 0) {
98:         return { ok: false, output: null, durationMs, logs: stderr.slice(0, 2000) };
99:       }
100: 
101:       let output: unknown = null;
102:       try {
103:         const raw = await readFile(join(tmpDir, "result.json"), "utf-8");
104:         output = JSON.parse(raw);
105:       } catch {
106:         return { ok: false, output: null, durationMs, logs: `No valid result.json. ${stderr.slice(0, 1000)}` };
107:       }
108: 
109:       return { ok: true, output, durationMs, logs: stderr };
110:     } finally {
111:       await rm(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }).catch(() => {});
112:     }
113:   }

<content>1: const BUFFER_SIZE = 200;
2: const CLEANUP_DELAY_MS = 30_000;
3: 
4: interface ChannelState {
5:   buffer: string[];
6:   listeners: Set<(line: string) => void>;
7:   doneListeners: Set<() => void>;
8:   completed: boolean;
9: }
10: 
11: /**
12:  * In-memory pub/sub for streaming log lines keyed by changeId.
13:  * Late subscribers receive buffered history immediately.
14:  */
15: export class LogBus {
16:   private channels = new Map<number, ChannelState>();
17: 
18:   private ensure(changeId: number): ChannelState {
19:     let ch = this.channels.get(changeId);
20:     if (!ch) {
21:       ch = { buffer: [], listeners: new Set(), doneListeners: new Set(), completed: false };
22:       this.channels.set(changeId, ch);
23:     }
24:     return ch;
25:   }
26: 
27:   emit(changeId: number, line: string): void {
28:     const ch = this.ensure(changeId);
29:     ch.buffer.push(line);
30:     if (ch.buffer.length > BUFFER_SIZE) {
31:       ch.buffer.splice(0, ch.buffer.length - BUFFER_SIZE);
32:     }
33:     for (const fn of ch.listeners) fn(line);
34:   }
35: 
36:   /**
37:    * Subscribe to log lines for a change.
38:    * Immediately replays buffered lines, then streams new ones.
39:    * Returns an unsubscribe function.
40:    */
41:   subscribe(
42:     changeId: number,
43:     onLine: (line: string) => void,
44:     onDone: () => void,
45:   ): () => void {
46:     const ch = this.ensure(changeId);
47: 
48:     // Replay buffer
49:     for (const line of ch.buffer) onLine(line);
50: 
51:     // If already completed, signal done immediately
52:     if (ch.completed) {
53:       onDone();
54:       return () => {};
55:     }
56: 
57:     ch.listeners.add(onLine);
58:     ch.doneListeners.add(onDone);
59: 
60:     return () => {
61:       ch.listeners.delete(onLine);
62:       ch.doneListeners.delete(onDone);
63:     };
64:   }
65: 
66:   /** Signal that the log stream for this change is finished. */
67:   complete(changeId: number): void {
68:     const ch = this.channels.get(changeId);
69:     if (!ch) return;
70:     ch.completed = true;
71:     for (const fn of ch.doneListeners) fn();
72:     // Clean up after a delay so late-connecting clients can still get the buffer
73:     setTimeout(() => this.channels.delete(changeId), CLEANUP_DELAY_MS);
74:   }
75: }

input.title: Add Codex summary streaming and diff review UI
input.what_changed: Add a reusable repo task runner, prompt builder, log bus, and typed summary annotations so Codex summaries run inside Docker and return validated JSON. Update the server and worker pipeline to support summary regeneration, raw diff retrieval, SSE log streaming, and re-entering `summarizing` from `ready_for_review`. Rework the change detail UI to inject route-specific header content and show annotated summaries, live Codex logs, and an interactive file tree plus rendered patch diff. Pin the Codex runner image to `@openai/codex@0.117.0`, write results through `/output/result.json`, and add new diff/tree rendering dependencies and shared global styles.
input.risk_assessment: `web/src/routes/change.tsx` now renders `<Timeline>` without importing or defining it anywhere in `web/src`, which is a likely frontend build blocker. The new summary regeneration and log streaming path can also leave a change stuck in `summarizing` if summary generation fails before any log line creates a `LogBus` channel, because `complete()` becomes a no-op and no recovery transition is added.
